Jobs and crons are plain package functions with dependencies as
parameters and (for jobs) the message last; they import nothing from the
jobs package. The directive resolves jobs.Store by path (the app imports
jobs only in its provider), assembles one *jobs.Manager, and generates
the in-memory Register/On/RegisterCron wiring (schedule persistence
happens later, when the app starts the scheduler).
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli => CLIDIR

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs => JOBSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- shared/shared.go --
package shared

import (
	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"
)

type Store struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewStore() *Store { return &Store{} }

//fabrik:provider
func NewJobStore() (jobs.Store, error) { return jobs.NewMemoryStore(), nil }

//fabrik:cli:command
func Boot(ctx cli.Context, mgr *jobs.Manager) error { return nil }
-- mail/mail.go --
package mail

import (
	"context"

	"app/shared"
)

type Welcome struct{ UserID int64 }

//fabrik:job
func SendWelcome(ctx context.Context, s *shared.Store, m Welcome) error { return nil }

type OrderPlaced struct{ ID int }

//fabrik:job name=email-receipt
func EmailReceipt(ctx context.Context, s *shared.Store, m OrderPlaced) error { return nil }

//fabrik:job name=update-inventory
func UpdateInventory(ctx context.Context, m OrderPlaced) error { return nil }

//fabrik:cron name=nightly schedule="0 3 * * *"
func Nightly(ctx context.Context, s *shared.Store) error { return nil }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "net/http"

//fabrik:http GET /{$}
func Home(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"syscall"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"
	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs"

	"app/mail"
	"app/shared"
)

func run() int {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	defer cancel()
	sigc := make(chan os.Signal, 2)
	signal.Notify(sigc, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
	defer signal.Stop(sigc)
	go func() {
		<-sigc
		cancel()
		<-sigc
		os.Exit(1)
	}()

	root := &cli.Command{
		Name: "app",
		Subcommands: []*cli.Command{
			{
				Name: "boot",
				Run: func(ctx cli.Context) error {
					manager, err := buildBoot(ctx)
					if err != nil {
						return err
					}
					return shared.Boot(ctx, manager)
				},
			},
		},
	}
	return root.Exec(os.Args[1:], cli.WithSignalContext(ctx))
}

func buildBoot(ctx context.Context) (*jobs.Manager, error) {
	// Providers
	sharedJobsStore, err := shared.NewJobStore()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	jobsManager, err := jobs.New(sharedJobsStore, jobs.Config{})
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	sharedStore := shared.NewStore()

	// Register
	// Jobs
	if err := jobs.Register[mail.OrderPlaced](jobsManager, "mail.OrderPlaced"); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if err := jobs.Register[mail.Welcome](jobsManager, "mail.Welcome"); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if err := jobs.On[mail.Welcome](jobsManager, "SendWelcome", func(c jobs.Context, m mail.Welcome) error {
		return mail.SendWelcome(c, sharedStore, m)
	}); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if err := jobs.On[mail.OrderPlaced](jobsManager, "email-receipt", func(c jobs.Context, m mail.OrderPlaced) error {
		return mail.EmailReceipt(c, sharedStore, m)
	}); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if err := jobs.On[mail.OrderPlaced](jobsManager, "update-inventory", func(c jobs.Context, m mail.OrderPlaced) error {
		return mail.UpdateInventory(c, m)
	}); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if err := jobs.RegisterCron(jobsManager, "nightly", "0 3 * * *", func(c jobs.Context) error {
		return mail.Nightly(c, sharedStore)
	}); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return jobsManager, nil
}
